     The Numbers Business Report

Into the Wild (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $18,354,356Details
International Box Office $38,468,604
Worldwide Box Office $56,822,960
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $16,902,924
Total $16,902,924
Further financial details...

Synopsis

An adaptation of the 1996 non-fiction book of the same name by Jon Krakauer based on the travels of Christopher McCandless across North America and his life spent in the Alaskan wilderness in the early 1990s.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$212,440 (1.2% of total gross)
Legs:8.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/660 max. theaters, 8.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $31,812,631

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 21st, 2007 (Limited) by Paramount Vantage
International Releases: January 14th, 2021 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 4th, 2008 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language and some nudity.
Running Time: 149 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue, Prologue
Geographical setting: Alaska
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Journey of Discovery, Non-Chronological, Road Trip, Runaway
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1990s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Square One Entertainment, Linson Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

An adaptation of the 1996 non-fiction book of the same name by Jon Krakauer based on the travels of Christopher McCandless across North America and his life spent in the Alaskan wilderness in the early 1990s.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$212,440 (1.2% of total gross)
Legs:8.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/660 max. theaters, 8.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $31,812,631

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 21st, 2007 (Limited) by Paramount Vantage
International Releases: January 14th, 2021 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 4th, 2008 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language and some nudity.
Running Time: 149 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue, Prologue
Geographical setting: Alaska
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Journey of Discovery, Non-Chronological, Road Trip, Runaway
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1990s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Square One Entertainment, Linson Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.
